channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: In Pictures: Hunger, despair as Venezuela quake death toll rises
item.description: Venezuelan rescuers fight to save lives as survivors face hunger and chaos after devastating twin earthquakes.

item.title: Spain vs Austria: World Cup Round of 32 – Preview, predictions, team news
item.description: Spain take on Austria in Los Angeles, with the winner facing either Portugal or Croatia in the last 16.

item.title: More than 900 arrested during South Africa’s antimigrant protests
item.description: Wave of arrests made across South Africa as xenophobic tensions erupt into nationwide marches.

item.title: Maradona to Messi: Why Bangladesh loves Argentina’s footballers
item.description: Diego Maradona turned a nation 17,000km from Argentina into die-hard fans. Lionel Messi has enthralled a new generation.

item.title: China’s new ethnic unity law extends its legal reach overseas
item.description: Rather than promote ethnic harmony, activists say the law could justify transnational repression.

item.title: Businessman on trial in Malta over prominent journalist’s murder
item.description: Yorgen Fenech is charged with orchestrating the assassination of journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia.

item.title: Taiwan needs to become a ‘hornet’s nest’ of drones, US diplomat says
item.description: De ​facto US ambassador Raymond Greene says drones represent a 'game-changing opportunity' to enhance Taiwan's security.

item.title: Belgium fans erupt after latest-ever goal seals place in last 16
item.description: Belgium fans celebrate a dramatic 3-2 victory over Senegal in extra time.

item.title: Oil prices fall to levels not seen since start of US-Israel war on Iran
item.description: Brent falls below $71 a barrel amid reports of progress in talks to end the war permanently.

item.title: Survivors look to foreign aid after the earthquakes in Venezuela
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Teresa Bo reports from a golf course turned relief centre in Venezuela.

item.title: California gets Bruce Lee Day in a first for US state’s Chinese Americans
item.description: Bruce Lee Day aims to honour the San Francisco-born martial arts legend as a cultural bridge and Asian-American icon.

item.title: US designates Ecuador’s Chone Killers gang as ‘terrorist’ organisation
item.description: Ecuador's government praises the US for backing President Noboa's campaign to dismantle gang-crime networks.

item.title: German prosecutors charge Ukrainian suspect over Nord Stream explosions
item.description: Prosecutors allege a yacht was used in the sabotage of pipelines, with the suspect leading the operation.

item.title: Lawyer takes Trump to task over unchecked presidential powers
item.description: Constitutional lawyer Bruce Fein says the US was founded on the principle that governments exist to protect rights.

item.title: EU border rules causing travel chaos ahead of summer peak, industry warns
item.description: European airlines and airports call for flexibility to suspend digital border system amid severe delays.

item.title: On the front line of Congo’s Ebola outbreak
item.description: Al Jazeera's Catherine Wambua-Soi reports from Bunia, on the front line of the Ebola response.

item.title: USA beat Bosnia 2-0 despite Balogun red card, enter World Cup last 16
item.description: Balogun scored USA's first goal and was sent off for a foul, but the home team scored another to advance to next round.

item.title: At least 13 killed in Kyiv as Zelenskyy warns of ‘massive Russian strike’
item.description: Dozens also injured as Ukraine's forces defend against ballistic missiles, drone barrage targeting capital.

item.title: Ocean temperatures hit record highs as El Niño looms
item.description: The world’s oceans are under heat stress, with average sea surface temperatures hitting 21°C.

item.title: ‘A war zone’: Venezuela aid workers fear health crisis after earthquakes
item.description: Aid workers fear disease outbreaks in temporary shelters amid overcrowding, lack of access to clean water, sanitation.

item.title: Belgium stage 3–2 comeback win over Senegal to enter World Cup last 16
item.description: Tielemans's penalty late into stoppage time of extra time capped Belgium's comeback from 2-0 down in Seattle.

item.title: Iran war live: ‘Positive progress’ as US, Tehran wrap up indirect talks
item.description: Tehran says 'communication channel' to be established with Washington to discuss breaches of the interim deal.

item.title: Couple arrested after daring Empire State marriage proposal stunt
item.description: Russian daredevils Vanya Beerkus and Angela Nikolau are seen as ambassadors for 'rooftopping', a climbing sport.

item.title: US judge sides with NAACP over proposed mail-in ballot restrictions
item.description: President Trump has sought to limit mail-in voting and has ordered his administration to impose limits on the practice.

item.title: Iran football team welcomed home after World Cup exit
item.description: Iran’s football team has landed back in Tehran after their World Cup
